start.text: You're really passionate about this job...
I'm indeed better at handling war... but not as a warrior.
So... which ship are you on this time?
I'll try to meet your expectations... probably.
Even light-years away, this warship and I are still in sync.
Fortunately, I don't hate this kind of killing that's like an electronic game...

start_boss.text: Let me clarify... I'm several light-years away...
Don't worry... you won't die, just end up half-dead at most.
Perhaps some people would be quite excited about this?
How interesting... after all, neither of us will truly pay with our lives.
Anyway, there are no real living people on this ship...

retreat.text: Is this really necessary? It's just another repair bill...
I'll leave the rest to Eden...
Are you sure you want a person far away to retreat?
Looks like you're not as wealthy as I imagined?
Preserving combat strength is the nature of Persean Sector inhabitants?

out_of_missiles.text: This is why I would never join such a design...
Ha... so these things were actually on this ship?

engaged.text: Casualties on only one side is extremely unfair.
How nostalgic, reminiscing about developing destructive magic...
Engagement confirmed... though I mostly play the role of a staff officer.
Hope your ship's weapons are more lethal than my magic.
Sadly... this seemingly life-or-death bloody exercise is just a game.

need_help.text: Ready to pay a repair bill? Maybe you can get reimbursement from Crimson City...?
Every command comes with corresponding consequences... you should have known that long ago?
Don't forget, being honored as the Primordial Witch doesn't mean I'm particularly good at remote control...
Don't try to waste effort saving an unmanned ship, this is advice from the Primordial Witch.

pursuing.text: Usually, the battle ends just by me standing in place...
Looks like even I need to move this time?
If it were me, I'd prefer ending this cat-and-mouse game with long-range weapons.
Before a light lance, whether you move in a straight or curved line makes no practical difference.
Do you want to recover this ship... or should I just destroy it?

running.text: Your ship seems more suited for this kind of thing.
I prefer defense over maneuverability... you know that.
Next time, you should find me a more solid ship...
Your ship is slowly moving, while I'm just sitting in a chair, spacing out...

hull_90.text: Normally, damage would already be tallied... too bad this ship is in my hands.
After the shield reaches critical point... taking some damage is only natural.
You must be curious how these damages get repaired without crew? Honestly, I'm not quite sure either...

hull_50.text: Looks like there's no difference as long as the health bar isn't empty... really lacking realism.
Normally, combat capability should be declining at this point, right? Strange...
Does this warship have any half-health berserk mechanism...? Probably not.
Things are getting interesting... would a normal person really say that?

hull_30.text: Ha... hope you'll find me a more robust ship next time...
Seems like the Persean Sector doesn't have any damage control magic...
Maybe it's time for a cup of tea... the battle's almost over anyway.
Have you prepared a second unit? Probably not...

overload.text: Feels a bit like an energy bar hitting rock bottom?
Perhaps disappointing you... I don't actually feel anything substantial.
Though the Wraith link lets me merge with the ship, it doesn't mean I'll actually get electrocuted...
Wonder if you have any weird hobbies?

death.text: Now you should worry about yourself, after all, it's not me who'll get hurt...
Don't worry... at least no one on this ship has insurance.
Eden told me your battlefield command scored zero.

victory.text: Hope next time you'll find me at a less private moment...
If I were directly commanding, it might have ended earlier.
Should I say you did well? At least for your skill level, it's acceptable.
